2. थाना स्तर पर शांति भंग की आशंका के आधार पर CrPC की धाराओं के तहत उसे पाबंद करने की माँग की जा सकती है।
3. स्कूल प्रधानाचार्य और ग्राम पंचायत को लिखित जानकारी देकर दूरी बनाए रखने की स्थानीय व्यवस्था कराई जाए।
4. बाल कल्याण समिति और DLSA से मिलकर परिवार के लिए दीर्घकालिक सुरक्षा और पुनर्वास योजना बनवाएँ।

Main ek NGO ke through kaam kar raha hoon. Ek case hai jahan [Accused] teacher ne allegedly 14-year-old boy ko Yatheemkhana me long period tak abuse kiya, similar to Crime No. 229/2019. Ab accused ne Section 438 CrPC anticipatory bail file ki hai High Court me, VC se sunwai ho rahi hai. Question yeh hai: POCSO Section 19 ka mandatory reporting is situation me ab bhi trigger hota hai, jab kuch incidents 2012 se pehle ke bhi hain? Aur bail proceedings me child ki identity aur confidentiality kaise protect hogi?

Haan, Section 19 POCSO ka mandatory reporting ab bhi apply karega, kyunki abhi aapko ek “child” (under 18 at time of offence) ke against sexual offence ki information hai. Jo acts 2012 ke baad huye, woh clearly POCSO ke under aayenge; 2012 se pehle wale parts IPC 377 ke under consider ho sakte hain.

Confidentiality ke liye: POCSO Sections 23, 24, 33(7) ke hisaab se identity publish nahi ho sakti. Court se request kar sakte hain:
- in-camera proceedings,
- child ka naam anonymise,
- VC hearing me screen name/pseudonym,
- records ko sealed rakhne ka order.

Ab investigation stage me child ka statement kaise record hoga? Section 161 CrPC police statement, aur Section 164 CrPC magistrate statement ke beech kya strategy honi chahiye, specially POCSO Section 24 child‑friendly norms ke context me? Kya IO aur Magistrate dono ko POCSO guidelines follow karni hoti hain? Aur NGO support person ke roop me, kya main child ke saath 164 statement ke waqt present rehne ki request kar sakta hoon? Kya bail hearing me child ko personally aana padega?

Process normally aise hoga:
- Section 161 CrPC: IO child ka statement record karega, POCSO Section 24 ke child‑friendly norms follow karte hue (ghar ya safe jagah, plain clothes, relative/guardian ki presence, no aggressive questioning, etc.).
- Section 164 CrPC: Magistrate ke saamne voluntary statement; yahan bhi POCSO norms apply hote hain.

NGO ko CWC/SJPU ke through “support person” appoint karwaya ja sakta hai; us base par aap presence request kar sakte hain.

Anticipatory bail hearing me aam taur par child ko personally appear nahi karaya jata; court case diary/material dekh kar decide karti hai.

Is case me Sections 377 IPC aur 3/4 POCSO dono lagaye gaye hain. Post‑POCSO scenario me, specially child victim ke cases me, kya 377 IPC add karna ab bhi zaroori/standard practice hai? Charge framing ke time, CrPC Section 216 ke context me, court kaise deal karega—alternative charges, ya jo graver offence hai uske hisaab se? Aur jo pre‑2012 period hai alleged abuse ka, uske liye sirf 377 IPC apply karega, correct?

Child cases me ab primary framework POCSO hi hai, kyunki yeh special law hai. Phir bhi practice me police aksar 377 IPC bhi add karte hain, especially jab period long ho, ya kuch part POCSO se pehle ka ho.

Pre‑2012 incidents: POCSO retrospectively apply nahi hota, isliye un parts ke liye mainly IPC 377 use hoga.

Charge framing ke waqt court POCSO aur 377 dono ke under charges frame kar sakta hai (even alternative form me). Conviction ke time double punishment nahi hoga; court graver/more specific provision ko prefer karegi.

Ab practical concern yeh hai ki Yatheemkhana/madrasa management se [Victim] ko intimidation ka risk hai. Kya Witness Protection Scheme 2018 is case me formally invoke ki ja sakti hai, ya sirf POCSO Sections 19(5), 33(7) par rely karna hoga? Agar humko request file karni ho, to kaunse court ke saamne karein (Special POCSO Court/High Court)? Aur protective measures me kya‑kya specifically request kiya ja sakta hai—residential transfer, no‑contact order, etc.?

Witness Protection Scheme 2018 Supreme Court‑approved hai, isliye poore India me applicable hai, including POCSO matters. Aap:
- Special POCSO Court ke saamne, PP ke through Witness Protection application file kar sakte hain; grave risk ho to High Court bhi approach kar sakte hain.

Possible measures:
- identity non‑disclosure, in‑camera trial,
- police protection,
- school/hostel transfer,
- [Accused] ke liye no‑contact / area restriction orders,
- contact details confidential rakhna.

Saath hi POCSO Sections 19(5), 33(7), 24, 37 cite karke child‑friendly protective orders bhi maang sakte hain.

Anticipatory bail arguments ke context me, kya complainant/victim side ka separate counsel POCSO matters me independent submissions de sakta hai, ya strictly PP ke through hi jaana hota hai? CrPC Sections 301/302 aur POCSO Section 40 ke hisaab se victim ke advocate ki kya role/limits hoti hain? Aur bail oppose karte waqt key points kaun‑se highlight karne chahiye—position of trust (teacher), long duration, possibility of influencing child witnesses, etc.?

POCSO Section 40 read with CrPC Section 301 ke hisaab se victim/complainant apna advocate engage kar sakta hai. Normally:
- Private counsel PP ko “assist” karta hai;
- Court permission se written submissions ya brief oral arguments allow kiye ja sakte hain, lekin primary conduct PP ke paas hi rehta hai.

Anticipatory bail oppose karte waqt emphasize kar sakte hain:
- serious nature of offence under POCSO/377,
- long‑term, repeated abuse allegation,
- teacher‑student relationship (position of trust),
- possibility of threat/intimidation and evidence tampering,
- child ka psychological impact,
- need for custodial interrogation.

Conditional bail me strict no‑contact, area restriction, institution se door rehna, witness protection measures insist kar sakte hain.

please help… my brother is in jail now 7 years… case of small girl in our village… judge said some special child law and also said like rape even though my brother says he did not do full act, only tried… we are very scared, don’t understand… why so big punishment when nothing fully happened… can we do anything now or is it finished…

I am sorry your family is going through this. In law, even “trying” to force sex can be a serious crime. That is called an attempt and can be punished under Section 376 read with Section 511 of the IPC. When the victim is a child, the special child law (POCSO Act) applies, for example Sections 3 and 4. The judge can also add threat charges (Section 506 IPC). You may still file an appeal in the High Court. If you cannot afford a lawyer, ask the Legal Services Authority for free help.

the girl is now bigger… her family also wants to finish matter, they say they dont want to continue… if she says in court now that nothing happened, will my brother come out fast… can we make some written compromise paper with them… i am worried also for that girl, i dont want trouble for her…

In child sexual cases, it is not a private compromise. The case is State versus the accused. Even if families agree, the court must still see the evidence. Under the POCSO Act (for example Sections 3, 4 and 17) and IPC Sections 376/511 and 506, the offence is very serious. Forcing the girl to change her statement can itself be an offence. Instead, speak to your lawyer about filing an appeal or sentence reduction. Make sure the [Victim] is safe and supported; she or her family can also contact Childline 1098 for help.
Sir please help… I got some paper saying the men who hurt my child have put one criminal appeal against order of special court. I am very scared. What does this mean? Will my child’s case stop now?

I am sorry your child and you are going through this. The appeal usually means the accused are challenging one order of the special court, often about bail or some interim order. It does NOT stop the main case about the abuse. The case for your child continues in the special POCSO court. In the higher court, the government lawyer will also speak. You can tell the prosecutor about your fear so they can inform the judge about the risks to your child.

Police told me they did very bad things, also gave her some drink, and said many serious cases are there. Now if they go to this higher court, can they come out and come near our house? I am from SC community, I am very afraid.

From what you say, police may have added serious offences under the POCSO Act for sexual assault, giving intoxicated substances, and also under the law protecting SC/ST persons. Because these are serious, courts are careful before giving bail. If they are already in jail, the appeal may be about bail. You can request the prosecutor to oppose their release and ask for strict conditions: no contact with you, no coming near your home or your child’s school. Tell the court clearly about your fear.
They use big words… police said something like aggravated, cruelty, and all. I don’t understand. My child is small, will judge believe her? She is scared to talk.

“Aggravated” usually means the assault is more serious, for example because the child is very young, was given intoxication, or suffered cruelty. Under the POCSO Act (like Sections 3–6, 10, 17), the law treats such acts very strictly. The judge can record your child’s statement in a child‑friendly way, often in private (in‑camera). Based on general legal rules, courts take a child’s testimony seriously if it is consistent. It is not your child’s fault; she only needs to tell the truth as she remembers.
Hearing is coming soon in this appeal. I don’t have money for big lawyer. Do I need my own lawyer there? How do I talk in that court, I don’t know anything.

You are not alone in this. In criminal cases, the State prosecutor usually argues for your side. Still, you have a right to support. You can: 
- Approach the District Legal Services Authority for a free legal aid lawyer.
- Ask for a “support person” under POCSO to guide you.
- Tell the prosecutor your fears so they can place them before the judge. 
You can also request victim compensation and protection measures through your legal aid lawyer.
My child cannot sleep, has nightmares, cries if we talk about court. I also feel guilty and confused. What can I do now so she is safe and does not suffer more because of this case?

Your feelings are understandable, and none of this is your or your child’s fault. For safety and support you can:
- Ask police or the prosecutor for protection measures and to keep your address confidential.
- Request that her statement be taken in a safe place, with a trusted person present, as allowed under POCSO (for example, Section 24).
- Contact Childline 1098 for counselling and local services.
- Ask the Child Welfare Committee or District Child Protection Unit for psychological support and a support person.
If court still gives them bail, what can I do? I am very scared they will threaten us or try to take revenge. Can I complain again if they come near us?

If bail is granted, the court can put strict conditions: no contact with you, no entering your area, no contacting witnesses. Tell the prosecutor to request these. If, after bail, they threaten or approach you:
- Immediately complain to the local police station.
- Inform the prosecutor and your legal aid lawyer.
- They can ask the court to cancel bail.
Because you are from an SC community and your child is a minor, both POCSO and the SC/ST Act allow the court to take threats very seriously.

statutes_cited: Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012 Sections 3-6, 10, 17, 19, 24
Scheduled Castes and the Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989 Section 3(1)(w)(i)
Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015

turns.text: Sir please help, kal CBI wale ghar aaye the. Mere bhai [Accused] ka phone aur ek hard disk le gaye, bol rahe the ki bachchon ke gande videos group me share hue hain. Mujhe kuch samajh nahi aa raha, ye itna serious hai kya? Bhai bol raha hai bas forward hua tha, usne banaya nahi. Main bahut dar gaya hoon.

Main samajh sakta hoon aap kitne tense honge. Haan, child sexual abuse videos circulate / store karna bahut serious offence hai. Is type ke cases generally POCSO Act ke provisions (jaise Section 13–15) aur IT Act Section 67B ke under aate hain, kabhi‑kabhi IPC bhi lag sakta hai. CBI digital evidence jaise phone, hard disk examine karegi. Abhi sabse pehle aapko ek criminal lawyer se milna chahiye jo CBI / cyber crime matters handle karta ho. Agar kisi real [Victim] child ka pata ho, uski safety ko priority dein.

Par sir agar usne sirf WhatsApp group me aaya hua video dekh liya ya aage bhej diya, to bhi utni hi badi problem hogi? Use lag raha hai ki bas admin ki galti hai. Jail ho sakti hai kya? Aur kya bachche ke saath jo hua uske liye bhi hum pe case aa sakta hai?

Law ke hisaab se sirf forward / store karna bhi offence hai. POCSO Section 15 aur IT Act Section 67B me child sexual abuse material ka possession, download, share karna punishable hai, chahe aapne video banaya ho ya nahi. Punishment generally jail + fine ho sakta hai, duration facts pe depend karega. Agar aapka bhai khud kisi [Victim] ke sexual abuse me involve nahi tha, to POCSO Section 3–10 ya IPC 354, 376 jaise sections shayad directly uspe na lagen, lekin exact position file mein evidence dekh ke lawyer batayega.

CBI wale bol rahe the phone me kaafi videos mile hain. Kya court me sabko dikhayenge? Family ko bhi bulayenge kya? Aur agar koi minor child identify ho gaya to uska kya hoga, usko court me ana padega kya? Mujhe us bachche ke liye bhi bura lag raha hai.

Aapka concern sahi hai. Court generally aise videos open court me nahi chalati; in‑camera proceedings hoti hain, child‑friendly process follow hota hai. POCSO Section 24, 33, 36, 37 ke hisaab se [Victim] child ka statement child‑friendly tariqe se hota hai, zaroori hua to video‑conferencing, screen, support person ke saath. Family members sirf witness ke roop me bulae ja sakte hain, agar unke paas relevant information ho. Investigation agencies try karti hain ki child ko minimum trauma ho, counselling aur protection measures bhi available hote hain.

Sir ek aur baat, group me bahut log the, kuch bahar ke country ke bhi. Kya main CBI ko unka detail de sakta hoon? Ya online complaint karni hogi? Mujhe darr lagta hai par galat log bach na jayein. Mere naam se koi dikkat to nahi hogi?

Bilkul, aap un sab logon / admins ki information CBI investigator ko de sakte hain, ye helpful hoga. Aap national cyber crime portal (cybercrime.gov.in) par bhi child sexual abuse material ki online complaint kar sakte hain. POCSO Section 19 reporting ko encourage karta hai; aap jaise log jo information rakhte hain, woh report kar sakte hain. Aapka statement witness ke taur pe record ho sakta hai, lekin law aapko offender jaisa treat nahi karega sirf isliye ki aapne help ki. Agar dar lag raha ho to lawyer ke through bhi details de sakte hain.

Abhi turant mujhe kya karna chahiye? CBI ne bulaaya to kya bolun, lawyer ke bina jana theek hai? Aur ghar me ek minor [Relative] bhi hai, usko bhi tension ho rahi hai. Uske liye koi help ya counselling mil sakti hai kya free me?

Steps yeh ho sakte hain: 1) Jaldi se ek experienced criminal / cyber lawyer engage karein, ideally CBI enquiry me uske saath hi jayein. 2) Evidence chhupane / delete karne ki koshish bilkul na karein, varna alag offence ban sakta hai. 3) Ghar ke minor [Relative] ke liye counselling chahiye ho to Childline 1098 par call karein; free support mil sakta hai. 4) Agar paise ki dikkat ho to District Legal Services Authority se free legal aid lawyer maang sakte hain. Har baat sach‑sach lawyer ko batayein.

We are supporting a family where a baby girl was admitted with suspected "battered baby syndrome" and the duty doctor informed the police, similar to the case where the court later convicted under Section 6 POCSO with 25 years RI and compensation. I want to clarify the hospital’s legal duties here. Under Section 19 POCSO, is the reporting by the doctor mandatory on mere suspicion, and once the written intimation is given, can the police straightaway register FIR adding IPC Section 376AB and POCSO Sections 5/6 based only on that note?

Yes, under Section 19 POCSO, any person, including a doctor, must report even on reasonable suspicion of sexual offence against a child. The doctor need not prove the offence; suspicion based on clinical findings is enough. Once the written intimation reaches police, Section 154 CrPC requires registration of an FIR. The police may initially invoke IPC 376AB and POCSO Sections 5/6 based on the doctor’s description and provisional opinion. These sections can later be added, modified, or dropped after detailed medical reports and further investigation.

Thank you, that helps. We are also concerned about evidence, since the child is too young to speak. What is the evidentiary value of the doctor’s note diagnosing battered baby syndrome and suspecting sexual abuse? In such a case, what additional procedures should be followed: medical examination under Section 27 POCSO, statements under CrPC, presence of parents or support persons, video-recording, etc.? How do courts usually assess cases where there is no direct testimony from the child, only medical and circumstantial evidence, including presumptions under Sections 29–30 POCSO?

The doctor’s note and final medical report are strong expert evidence. In cases where the child cannot speak, courts often rely on: (1) medical findings, (2) surrounding circumstances, (3) conduct of caretakers, and (4) the statutory presumptions under POCSO Sections 29–30, once basic foundational facts are shown. Procedures should include: immediate detailed medical examination (POCSO Section 27), safe environment, preferably a woman doctor, documentation and sample collection, 164 CrPC statements of caregivers, use of support persons, and avoidance of repeated interviewing (Section 24 POCSO).

In the case I mentioned, the trial court imposed 25 years RI under Section 6 POCSO plus fine and Rs. 4,00,000 compensation. From a legal standpoint, is such a sentence within the court’s discretion, given that Section 6 prescribes a minimum 20 years? How is the compensation quantum generally determined (POCSO Section 33(8), Rules, or victim compensation schemes)? Also, as an NGO, can we assist the family during any appeal process and coordinate with DLSA for enforcement or enhancement of compensation and support services?

Section 6 POCSO allows punishment of at least 20 years, which may extend to life; within that range, the judge can choose 25 years based on gravity, age of the child, and aggravating factors. Compensation is guided by POCSO Section 33(8), POCSO Rules (victim compensation) and state victim compensation schemes, considering medical costs, trauma, and long‑term needs. As an NGO, you can: help the family liaise with DLSA, support applications for compensation, arrange counselling and shelter, and assist the prosecution (with court’s permission) during appeal, while prioritising the child’s safety.

[Victim] की उम्र 14 साल है। पास के घरेलू शिक्षक ने कई बार उसे अकेले में गलत तरीके से छूने और गंदी बातें करने की कोशिश की। परिवार ने अभी तक सिर्फ़ मोहल्ले के कुछ लोगों को बताया है, पुलिस में नहीं गए, क्योंकि बदनामी और डर लग रहा है। क्या यह सीधे POCSO के तहत अपराध माना जाएगा? इसमें आम तौर पर कौन‑कौन सी धाराएं लग सकती हैं? क्या इस स्थिति में FIR ज़रूर दर्ज करनी चाहिए?

आपने जो अनुभव साझा किया, वह बहुत गंभीर है और आपका डर व चिंता दोनों उचित हैं। सबसे पहले, [Victim] की सुरक्षा और भावनात्मक सहारा सबसे ज़रूरी है।

चौदह वर्ष की बच्ची के साथ इस तरह का यौन स्पर्श या बातें, उसकी सहमति के बावजूद भी, कानूनन अपराध हैं। सामान्य रूप से यह POCSO अधिनियम की धाराओं 3 से 10 के अंतर्गत, और साथ में IPC की धाराएं 354 या 376 के अंतर्गत आ सकता है, घटना के अनुसार।

आप FIR दर्ज कराने और 1098 या नज़दीकी DLSA से मदद लेने पर गंभीरता से विचार कर सकते हैं।

यदि हम FIR दर्ज कराते हैं तो व्यावहारिक प्रक्रिया क्या होगी? कौन से थाने में जाना चाहिए, और यदि पुलिस लिखने से कतराए तो हम क्या कर सकते हैं? दूसरा, बच्ची बहुत डरती है कि बार‑बार घटना बतानी पड़ेगी और सब उसके सामने सवाल करेंगे। POCSO में क्या प्रावधान हैं जिससे उसका बयान सुरक्षित माहौल में हो और उस पर कम से कम मानसिक दबाव पड़े?

FIR आप किसी भी नज़दीकी पुलिस थाना या महिला थाना में दर्ज करा सकते हैं, क्षेत्राधिकार बाद में पुलिस तय करती है। यदि वहाँ उपस्थित अधिकारी लिखने से मना करें, तो थाने के प्रभारी, ज़िला पुलिस प्रमुख को लिखित शिकायत, या 1098 के माध्यम से सूचना दे सकते हैं। POCSO की धारा 19 ऐसी सूचना देने का कर्तव्य बताती है।

बयान के लिए POCSO की धारा 24 में बाल हितैषी पूछताछ का प्रावधान है; यथासंभव महिला अधिकारी, परिचित स्थान, विश्वसनीय व्यक्ति की उपस्थिति और बंद अदालत की कार्यवाही की व्यवस्था की जाती है।

मान लीजिए कुछ रिश्तेदार कह रहे हैं कि मामला शांत कर दो, आपस में बात करके समझौता कर लो या बाद में लड़की की शादी कर देंगे, पुलिस तक बात न ले जाओ। अगर परिवार दबाव में आकर समझौता कर ले और बाद में पछताए, तो क्या मुकदमा वापस लिया जा सकता है? और क्या ऐसे समझौते पर सहमति देना खुद भी किसी तरह का कानूनी जोखिम बना सकता है?

बाल लैंगिक शोषण से जुड़े अपराध सामान्यतः गंभीर माने जाते हैं और इनका निजी समझौता कानून के अनुसार मान्य नहीं होता। POCSO तथा संबंधित IPC धाराएं आम तौर पर राज्य बनाम अभियुक्त के रूप में चलती हैं, इसलिए पैसा लेकर या रिश्तेदारी के दबाव में समझौता करने से भी मुकदमा स्वतः समाप्त नहीं हो जाता।

यदि कोई परिवार को डराकर, धमकाकर समझौता करवाना चाहता है, तो यह IPC की धारा 506 जैसे अपराध बन सकता है। ऐसे हर दबाव को सुरक्षित रखें, पुलिस या न्यायालय को लिखित रूप से बताएं और DLSA से निःशुल्क वकील की सहायता लें।

I am a social worker with an NGO handling a case very similar to the Antony v. State matter, where the child’s date of birth is clearly mentioned, like 10.07.2004 in that case. In practice, how does the court legally prove the age of the child in POCSO cases – birth certificate, school records, medical opinion? And why is this age determination so critical for charges and sentencing under the POCSO Act?

Age is usually proved in this order of preference:
1. Birth certificate / municipal records
2. School admission register / transfer certificate
3. Hospital records of birth
4. Only if these are absent, medical age estimation.

Under POCSO, any person below 18 is a "child" (Section 2(1)(d)). Once age is proved, consent becomes legally irrelevant. The age fixes whether POCSO applies at all, and which sections (e.g., penetrative sexual assault under Sections 3–4, aggravated under 5–6), and it strongly influences minimum sentence and bail considerations.

In that case, the mother gave a written complaint which became the FIR. For our matter, the mother came first to our NGO. Under Section 19 POCSO, what is our mandatory reporting obligation as NGO staff? Is it enough to support her to file a written complaint at the police station, or must we independently report? Also, how should the child’s initial statement be handled to avoid re-traumatisation and still be usable later under CrPC?

Section 19 POCSO makes reporting mandatory for any person with knowledge of the offence, including NGO staff. Safest practice:

1. Immediately help the mother file a written complaint with local police/SJPU or call Childline 1098.
2. You may also send your own written information to the police, attaching her version.
3. Avoid detailed questioning of the child; only note essentials.
4. Police should record the child’s statement in a child-friendly manner (Section 24 POCSO, Section 161 CrPC) and arrange a Section 164 CrPC statement before a Magistrate as soon as possible.

In Antony’s case, apart from the sentence, the trial court ordered that most of the fine be paid to the child as compensation. For our case, could we seek both fine-based compensation from the Special Court and additional compensation under the Victim Compensation Scheme (Section 357A CrPC / POCSO Rules)? Practically, in Tamil Nadu, should we move an application before the Special Court, the DLSA, or both?

Yes, both are possible and often run together:

1. The Special Court can direct that fine be paid to the child as compensation (Section 357 CrPC) and also recommend/additional compensation under Section 357A CrPC and POCSO Rules.
2. In Tamil Nadu, you can:
   - Move an application before the Special Court during trial or at judgment, and
   - Approach the District Legal Services Authority (DLSA) for Victim Compensation.

The court’s recommendation usually helps the DLSA quantify and release compensation faster.

In Antony, the accused appealed the conviction. As support persons, how can we track and participate in the appeal? Under the proviso to Section 372 CrPC, does the child/victim have an independent right to appeal, for example, if compensation is inadequate? Or do we have to depend only on the State filing an appeal under Sections 377/378 CrPC?

You can monitor the appeal by:
- Obtaining the appeal number from the High Court registry.
- Regularly checking cause lists or engaging a lawyer/legal aid.

Under the proviso to Section 372 CrPC, the victim has an independent right to appeal against:
- Acquittal,
- Conviction for lesser offence,
- Inadequate compensation.

For enhancement of sentence, generally the State files under Section 377 CrPC, but the victim can still approach the High Court seeking appropriate relief and may request legal aid through the Legal Services Authority.

During trial, when [Victim] gives evidence, we are worried about secondary trauma. What safeguards should we insist on under POCSO and CrPC so she is not directly exposed to the accused or asked humiliating questions about her character? Can you point to the relevant provisions (e.g., Section 33 POCSO, Section 273 CrPC, Evidence Act) that we can cite before the Special Court?

You can request the Special Court to ensure:

1. In-camera proceedings and child-friendly atmosphere (Sections 33, 37 POCSO).
2. Child not seeing the accused directly—use of screens or separate seating, or video link (Section 36 POCSO, proviso to Section 273 CrPC).
3. No aggressive or character-assassinating questions (Section 33(5) POCSO).
4. No questions on past sexual history to show consent or character (Section 146 proviso and Section 53A Evidence Act).
5. Presence of a trusted support person and breaks during testimony, if needed.

statutes_cited: Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012: Sections 2(1)(d), 3, 4, 5, 6, 19, 21, 24, 33, 36, 37

turns.text: Main ek NGO ke through child survivor ko support kar raha hoon. Case mein allegation hai ki [Accused] ne [Victim] ko kidnap karke marne ki koshish ki, aur saath hi sexual nature ke acts ka doubt hai. Ab [Accused] ne Section 439 CrPC ke under bail petition file kiya hai, aur wife ko already bail mil chuki hai, so unka lawyer parity ka argument le raha hai. Aise facts mein, opposition side se bail oppose karne ke main grounds kya highlight karne chahiye, specially child safety ke context mein?

Aapka focus bilkul sahi hai, child safety sabse important hai.

Bail oppose karte time aap yeh points emphasize kar sakte hain:
1) Nature and gravity: Kidnapping + attempted murder (IPC 363, 307) + possible POCSO offences – highly serious.
2) Risk to child: Agar bail mile, [Accused] dwara [Victim] ko threaten/pressurise karne ka strong possibility, especially statement pending ho.
3) Parity limit: Wife ko bail milna automatic parity nahi deta; uska alleged role alag ho sakta hai.
4) POCSO presumption: Section 29 se seriousness aur victim‑centric approach highlight karein.

Thik hai. Ab ek doubt hai: mandatory reporting angle. Yahan sexual assault ka suspicion baad mein clear hua, FIR main kidnapping/attempt to murder pe pehle focus tha. POCSO Section 19 ke hisaab se mandatory reporting delay aur complaint filing delay ko lekar defence bail mein benefit maang raha hai. Kya hum argument bana sakte hain ki delay child psychology, fear, family pressure ki wajah se hua, aur bail stage par delay ko itna weight nahi milna chahiye?

Bilkul, aap yeh argument strongly le sakte hain.

Bail oppose karte waqt aap keh sakte hain:
- POCSO Section 19 mandatory reporting hai, par ground reality mein child aur family ko samajhne, trust build hone mein time lagta hai.
- Delay ko child trauma, fear, stigma aur accused ke influence se link karein.
- Courts ne baar‑baar maana hai ki sexual offences mein delay natural hai.
- Bail stage par court ko prima facie material, child disclosure aur risk assessment ko delay se upar rakhna chahiye.

Abhi [Victim] ka Section 164 CrPC statement record hona baaki hai. Guardian ko darr hai ki agar bail mil gaya to [Accused] pressure daalega, child wapas apni baat se mukar sakta hai. Kya hum court ko specifically request kar sakte hain ki jab tak 164 statement aur POCSO ke under child‑friendly recording complete na ho, tab tak bail na di jaye? Kaunse provisions cite karna useful hoga – POCSO Sections 24, 26, 33(7) etc.?

Haan, aap yeh targeted prayer rakh sakte hain.

Aap argue karein:
- Section 164 CrPC statement abhi record nahi hua, isliye accused ko bail dena witness‑tampering ka high risk create karega.
- POCSO Section 24, 26 child‑friendly, free‑from‑fear statement ka mandate dete hain.
- POCSO Section 33(7) expressly kehta hai ki accused ka child se direct contact avoid kiya jaye.
- CrPC Section 160 bhi bachchon ke liye protective hai. Isliye court ko pehle statement secure karne, fir bail consider karne ka request karein.

Medical examination mein major injuries nahi aayi, defence isi point ko use karke ‘false implication’ aur ‘no prima facie case’ ka ground bana raha hai bail ke liye. Opposition side se hum kaise explain karein ki injuries absent hone se bhi case weak nahi ho jata, especially kidnapping + attempt to murder + CSA suspicion scenario mein? Kya hum yeh keh sakte hain ki disclosure aur surrounding circumstances bail ke stage par kaafi hain?

Aap yeh clear position le sakte hain:

- Sexual and child offences mein medical injuries ka absence common hai; yeh settled law hai ki sirf is basis par case false nahi maan sakte.
- Kidnapping/attempt to murder (IPC 363, 307) ke liye bhi har baar visible injury zaroori nahi hoti; intention, circumstances aur recovery important hote hain.
- Bail stage par detailed appreciation nahi, sirf prima facie view hota hai.
- Victim’s consistent disclosure, circumstances of recovery, aur threat perception milkar bail reject karne ke liye sufficient material hain.

Mujhe ek case ke bare me samajhna hai. Ek 8 saal ki bachchi [Victim] ke saath uske father ke friend [Accused] ne sexual abuse kiya, ab police ne IPC 376 aur POCSO ke under case register kiya hai. Bachchi ki maa ko bhi second accused bana diya hai, bol rahe hain ki usne help ki, lodge leke gayi etc. Kya law ke hisaab se maa ko bhi aise accused bana sakte hain? Aur is situation me uske bail ka kya option hota hai?

Aise matters bahut sensitive hote hain, aapki tension samajh sakta hoon.

Law ke hisaab se, agar koi adult jaan-boojhkar help kare, plan kare ya protect kare offender ko, to use “abetment” maana ja sakta hai. POCSO Act me Section 16–17 (abetment) apply ho sakte hain, aur IPC me bhi abetment / conspiracy sections lag sakte hain.

Bail ke liye CrPC Section 439 ke tahat High Court / Sessions Court me bail application jaata hai. Court offence ki seriousness, maa ka actual role, evidence, aur bachchi ki safety sab dekhega.

Agar maa ne direct kuch nahi kiya, bas husband ya first accused ke pressure me bachchi ko lodge tak le gayi ho, par usne khud abuse nahi kiya, to kya uske khilaaf abetment prove karna mushkil hoga? Kya sirf child ka statement ki woh mummy ke saath gayi thi, itna kaafi hota hai usko second accused banane ke liye?

Abetment ke liye normally yeh dikhana padta hai ki maa ne jaan-boojhkar help ki ya offence ke bare me knowledge thi. Sirf accompany karna, bina samajhe kya ho raha hai, alag cheez ho sakti hai.

Child ka statement bahut important evidence hota hai (police ke saamne CrPC Section 161, aur Magistrate ke saamne Section 164). Lekin trial me cross-examination hoti hai, context dekha jaata hai.

Defence me yeh dikhaya ja sakta hai ki maa ko actual offence ka pata nahi tha, ya woh khud pressure / fear me thi. Legal aid lawyer lena helpful hoga.

Bail hearing me judge kin main cheezon ko dekhega, especially jab victim minor hai aur maa hi normally uski primary caregiver thi? Kya yeh point ki bachchi ko emotional support chahiye aur maa custody me hai, bail ke favour me ja sakta hai? Ya phir child protection ke liye alag se custody decide hoti hai?

Bail ke time judge mainly dekhega: offence ki gravity (POCSO + IPC 376 bahut serious), prima facie evidence, witness ko influence karne ka risk, aur investigation ki stage.

POCSO Section 29 se trial me presumption hota hai, lekin bail phir bhi judicial discretion hai.

Child ki safety ke liye custody ka decision usually Child Welfare Committee (CWC) / JJ Act ke under hota hai. Bachchi ko kabhi-kabhi relative, shelter home ya supervised care me rakha jaata hai. Court dekh sakta hai ki maa ke bahar aane se bachchi par koi risk ya pressure to nahi hoga.

Agar abhi tak child ka detailed statement clear nahi hai regarding maa ka role, to kya hum police se request kar sakte hain ki supplementary statement li jaye, jisme yeh clarify ho? Lekin mujhe concern hai ki baar‑baar statement se bachchi ko trauma hoga. Isko legally aur psychologically safe tarike se kaise handle karein?

Supplementary statement possible hai, lekin aapka trauma concern bilkul sahi hai.

POCSO Section 19, 24 ke hisaab se child‑friendly procedure follow hona chahiye: ideally ek hi baar mein maximum detail, female officer, familiar environment, guardian/support person, psychologist ki help.

Aap IO se likhit request de sakte hain ki agar clarification zaroori ho to specialist counsellor ke through, minimum baar questioning ho. Special POCSO Court se bhi guidance / directions maange ja sakte hain.

Saath hi, Childline 1098 ya local child psychologist se therapy ke liye contact karna helpful rahega.

Last doubt: agar police charge sheet me maa pe POCSO ka abetment wala section laga dete hain, to approximately maximum punishment kitni ho sakti hai? Aur kya aise cases me compromise ya settlement possible hota hai, ya sirf High Court me quashing jaise options hote hain?

POCSO Section 17 kehtaa hai ki jo abetment kare, use generally wahi punishment face karni pad sakti hai jo main offence ki hai. Agar allegation penetrative sexual assault / aggravated hai (Sections 4, 6), to minimum 10 saal se life imprisonment tak ho sakta hai.

POCSO aur IPC 376 type offences normally compoundable nahi hote, matlab simple compromise se case khatam nahi hota. Kabhi‑kabhi High Court CrPC Section 482 ke under exceptional circumstances me proceedings quash karta hai, lekin yeh rare aur facts‑specific hota hai. Strong legal defence prepare karna sabse important hai.

statutes_cited: Juvenile Justice Act (Child Welfare Committee provisions)

turns.text: Main ek NGO ke through ek matter dekh raha hoon jo Sani vs State of U.P. jaisa hi hai: BNS ke Sections 137(2), 65(1), 64(F), saath me 5N/6 POCSO aur Child Marriage Act Section 9 lage hue hain. Yahan [Victim] apni statement me keh rahi hai ki woh voluntarily [Relative] ke ghar gayi thi aur sexual assault deny kar rahi hai. Aise situation me, presumption of innocence (Article 21, POCSO Section 29) aur child protection ko kaise balance kiya jata hai, specially bail hearings me?

Aap jo concern raise kar rahe hain woh bilkul valid hai. Bail stage par court generally teen cheezein dekhta hai: prima facie material, flight risk, aur victim/witness ke safety concerns.

Sani jaisi cases me, jab [Victim] FIR ke allegations deny kare, tab bhi:
- Presumption of innocence + Article 21 se liberty favour hoti hai
- Par POCSO offences serious hain, isliye court victim protection ko bhi weight deta hai

Court bail dete waqt conditions laga sakta hai: no contact with [Victim], residence restriction, intimidation na karne ka specific direction, etc.

Assume karo ki yahan [Victim] ka Section 164 CrPC statement bhi FIR se contradict kar raha hai aur woh explicit denial de rahi hai. 1) Kya is stage par usko ‘hostile’ ke angle se dekhte hain ya sirf evidentiary contradiction maana jata hai? 2) NGO ki taraf se kya hum Special POCSO Court me suggest kar sakte hain ki bail conditions me [Accused] ko [Victim] se zero contact, school/ghar ke area se door rehne ka clause add ho, even though victim abhi support nahi kar rahi complaint ko?

Section 164 CrPC statement par:
1) “Hostile witness” ka concept trial ke time apply hota hai, jab prosecution usko cross-examine karwana chahe. Bail stage par court sirf dekh raha hota hai ki contradictory statements se prosecution case kitna weak/strong lag raha hai.

2) Haan, NGO as “victim support” ya “interested party” application de sakta hai, request kar sakte hain:
- No-contact with [Victim]/family
- [Victim] ke school, ghar, neighbourhood se specified distance
- Phone/social media se bhi koi contact nahi
Yeh sab victim safety ke liye reasonable conditions hain.

Agar High Court ne already bail grant kar di ho, Sani case ki tarah, lekin ground level pe hume lag raha ho ki [Victim] pe indirect pressure aa raha hai, to hum practically kya steps le sakte hain? Kya Special POCSO Court ke samne bail cancellation ya modification move karna better hoga (CrPC 437(5)/439(2))? Aur parallel me JJ Act ke under CWC ke paas protection, counselling, ya safe placement ke liye approach karna strategically sahi rahega?

Aap dono tracks parallel chala sakte hain:

1) Bail cancellation/modification:
- Special POCSO Court ya High Court me application under CrPC Section 437(5)/439(2)
- Grounds: bail misuse, intimidation, indirect pressure, conditions violation
- Prayer: bail cancel ho ya stricter conditions add hon (no-contact, locality restriction, periodic reporting to police)

2) Protection under JJ Act:
- CWC ko written report ki jiye, [Victim] ko “child in need of care and protection” batate hue
- Safe shelter, counselling, safety plan request
- Childline 1098 aur DLSA se bhi coordination useful rahega.

statutes_cited: Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015

turns.text: please help… my daughter is small girl… we went to doctor for stomach pain, they told she is pregnant… police case started… now I heard the man and his family are asking for bail in high court like some big case. I am very scared he will come out and trouble us. I am poor, don’t know law… what does this bail mean, will they leave him?

I am very sorry you and your child are going through this. Bail means the accused asks the court to be released from jail during the case, with conditions. In serious child sexual abuse matters (like under POCSO and rape sections of IPC), courts are very careful and look at the age of the child, pregnancy, and safety of the victim. You can tell the police and government lawyer that you are afraid. You may also call Childline 1098 for help and support. Your safety and your child’s safety are most important.

I dont know what to say in court… they say maybe judge will ask about bail… I am not educated… what should I tell so they understand our fear… and if he comes out, can he come near our house, school? I am so worried for my child, she cries all night.

You can simply speak in your own words. You may say:
- your child is a minor and now pregnant
- you are afraid of [Accused]
- if there were any threats earlier
- how your child is crying, not sleeping, scared.

These offences are usually under Section 376 IPC and Section 6 of the POCSO Act. The judge can put strict conditions: no visit to your village, no contact with you or [Victim], no going near her school. You can request police and the government lawyer to ask for such protection conditions.
will my child have to go again and again to police and court? last time she was crying so much, she is very small… I dont want her to suffer more. can I tell them to take statement only one time?

Under the POCSO Act, the system should be child-friendly. Law says:
- police must try to take the child’s statement at home or a safe place (Section 24)
- the trial should be in a closed courtroom, not in public (Section 33)
- unnecessary repeated questioning should be avoided.

You can request:
- that her main statement already given is used
- any further questions be asked gently, maybe through video or from another room
- presence of a support person or counsellor. Tell the judge clearly that repeated questioning is harming her.

police took her phone, there were some messages and photos… I feel very ashamed… I dont want everyone to see those things… but they say it is proof… will it be shown to everyone in court? can we stop it?

Those messages and photos can be important evidence, especially for online abuse or sharing, which may come under Section 67 of the IT Act and POCSO. But your child’s privacy must be protected.

You can request through the government lawyer:
- in-camera (closed) proceedings so the public cannot see
- that only the judge and necessary officers view the material
- strict ban on media publishing any detail, as POCSO Section 23 protects the identity of child victims.

You can also ask for free legal aid to help you put these requests.

I am the accused in a special POCSO case for alleged touching and harassment under IPC outraging modesty and POCSO provisions, similar to a case I saw from Jamnagar. The matter is before the Special POCSO Court in the district. I feel the local atmosphere is very hostile because I am a known social worker. Can I ask the High Court to transfer the case and have the trial held before the High Court itself, maybe under section 474 CrPC or some similar provision?

You are entitled to ask for transfer, but the court will grant it only in limited situations. Normally, POCSO cases are tried by the Special Court (Sessions level), not directly by the High Court. Section 474 CrPC deals with trials before the High Court, but it is rarely used. For transfer, you usually apply under the CrPC provisions on transfer of cases, showing concrete reasons: serious threat to a fair trial, local prejudice, or risk to safety. A lawyer can draft a detailed transfer application with evidence.

What kind of evidence or facts would the High Court expect to see to believe there is real prejudice in the district court? People in the town talk badly about me, but the judge has not said anything openly. Also, in my case some witnesses have already been examined. Does that make it harder to get a transfer at this stage?

Courts usually look for specific, provable facts, not only general fear. Examples: open threats, organised protests at court, influence of powerful local persons, clear media campaigns against you, or conduct by police or prosecution that shows bias. Affidavits, news reports, complaints to authorities, and any police protection applications can help. Yes, once several witnesses are examined, courts are more reluctant to transfer, as it delays the POCSO trial. But if you show serious risk to fairness or safety, the High Court can still consider it.

I am already on bail. The charges include IPC 354A and POCSO sections 8, 9 and 10. Are these normally bailable or non-bailable, and does that affect my transfer request? Also, while the transfer application is pending, what should be my legal strategy to protect myself and my reputation as a social worker?

IPC 354A is generally a bailable offence, but Sections 8, 9 and 10 of the POCSO Act are usually treated as non-bailable, though courts often grant bail depending on facts. Bail status does not stop you from seeking transfer, but it may reduce the urgency in the court’s view. Strategy: strictly follow all bail conditions, never contact [Victim] or witnesses, collect documents showing your prior good work, keep records of any threats or prejudice, and let your lawyer focus on cross-examination and inconsistencies in prosecution evidence.

Even though I am the accused, I am also worried about [Victim]’s comfort in court. Under POCSO, what child-friendly procedures can we insist on? Can we ask for in-camera proceedings and to keep [Victim]’s identity confidential from media and the public?

POCSO strongly protects child witnesses. Sections 33–37 POCSO and Section 327(2) CrPC allow in-camera proceedings; POCSO trials are generally not open to the public. Section 24 POCSO encourages child-friendly recording of statements, and the judge must ensure the child is not intimidated. Section 23 POCSO prohibits publication of identifying details of [Victim]. Through your lawyer, you can support applications for in-camera hearings, screens or video-link, and strict anonymity, which protect the child while still preserving your right to a fair defence.

statutes_cited: Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012, Sections 8, 9, 10, 19, 23, 24, 33, 37
Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015 (general child protection framework)

turns.text: मेरे रिश्तेदार [Accused] पर झगड़े के दौरान लकड़ी की डंडी से मारपीट और गाली‑गलौच का मामला दर्ज हुआ है। वही घटनाक्रम है जैसा हाल ही में एक उच्च न्यायालय के मामले में था, BNS की कई धाराएँ लगी हैं और वह लगभग तीन महीने से न्यायिक हिरासत में है। साथ में एक नाबालिग [Minor] भी मौजूद था, जिसे बाल न्याय बोर्ड भेजा गया है। कोई यौन शोषण नहीं हुआ, बस मारपीट और गंदी गालियाँ थीं। क्या इसमें POCSO लग सकता है? और क्या ऐसे में नियमित जमानत मिलना संभव है?

आपकी चिंता समझ में आती है, परिवार पर ऐसा मामला बहुत दबाव डालता है।
दी गई जानकारी के अनुसार, क्योंकि किसी प्रकार का यौन कृत्य या यौन छेड़छाड़ नहीं बताई गई, सामान्यत: POCSO लागू नहीं होता। यह कानून तब लगता है जब बच्चे के साथ यौन हमला, यौन छेड़छाड़ या अश्लील उद्देश्य से संपर्क हो।
मारपीट, गाली और धमकी जैसे आरोप BNS या IPC की धाराओं के अंतर्गत ही चलते हैं।
नियमित जमानत के लिए उच्च न्यायालय या सत्र न्यायालय में आवेदन किया जा सकता है, इसके लिए किसी स्थानीय अधिवक्ता से तुरंत सलाह लेना अच्छा रहेगा।

मान लीजिए उसी झगड़े के दौरान [Minor] के साथ गलत तरीके से शरीर पर हाथ लगाया गया हो, बच्चा उस समय डर गया और किसी को कुछ नहीं बताया। अब लगभग दो महीने बाद उसने रो‑रो कर यह बात बताई है। परिवार को बहुत अपराधबोध हो रहा है कि हमने पहले शिकायत नहीं की। अब हम क्या कर सकते हैं? क्या इतनी देरी के बाद भी POCSO के तहत FIR दर्ज हो सकती है या कानून में समय सीमा होती है?

बच्चे ने देर से बताया, यह बहुत सामान्य है, इसलिए खुद को दोष न दें।
POCSO में बच्चे के साथ यौन छेड़छाड़ या हमला पर आम तौर पर सूचना देने की कोई समय सीमा नहीं है। दो महीने बाद भी आप थाने में जाकर FIR दर्ज कराने की माँग कर सकते हैं या बाल कल्याण समिति तथा बाल सहायता सेवा 1098 से मदद ले सकते हैं।
POCSO धारा 19 के अनुसार किसी भी व्यक्ति को सूचना देने का अधिकार और जिम्मेदारी है। देरी का कारण स्पष्ट रूप से पुलिस बयान में लिखवाना महत्वपूर्ण रहेगा।

घर वाले अभी भी पुलिस से बहुत डर रहे हैं, कि वे बच्चे से सख्ती से पूछताछ करेंगे और झगड़े वाले पुराने मामले को हमारे ही खिलाफ मोड़ देंगे कि हमने सब मिलकर झूठा यौन शोषण बना दिया। क्या हम पहले किसी संस्था या अदालत से सहायता ले सकते हैं, ताकि बच्चे की सुरक्षा भी रहे और हमारे ऊपर पलटकर झूठे मामले न बनें?

आपकी आशंका वाजिब है, ऐसे में सावधानी से आगे बढ़ना ज़रूरी है।
आप सीधे थाने जाने से पहले जिला विधिक सेवा प्राधिकरण (DLSA) से निःशुल्क अधिवक्ता और मार्गदर्शन माँग सकते हैं, या बाल कल्याण समिति तथा महिला और बाल विकास कार्यालय में लिखित शिकायत दे सकते हैं और अनुरोध कर सकते हैं कि POCSO के अनुसार मित्रतापूर्ण माहौल में ही बच्चे का बयान हो।
अपना पक्ष विस्तार से लिखकर सुरक्षित रखें, यदि कोई धमकी मिले तो उसके बारे में भी अलग से सूचना दी जा सकती है।

Meri beti [Victim] ke saath sexual abuse hua tha, ab POCSO ka case Special Court me chal raha hai. Accused [Accused] aur uske log baar‑baar dhamki de rahe hain, ghar ke paas ghoomte hain. Police complaint diya tha par proper protection nahi mil raha. Maine ek High Court ka order dekha jahan mother ne police protection ke liye writ file kiya tha. Kya main bhi High Court me aisa writ petition daal sakti hoon for protection for me and [Victim]?

Aap bilkul High Court me writ of mandamus file kar sakti hain, jisme aap Superintendent of Police aur concerned police station se specific police protection maang sakti hain, jab tak POCSO trial khatam na ho.

POCSO Act ke under (specially Section 19, 24) police aur State ka duty hai child‑victim ko protect karna. Pehle aap written representation SP aur AWPS ko dijiye; uska copy, receipt, aur threat ki details High Court writ me annex kar sakti hain. Parallel me aap 1098 (Childline) aur 112 par emergency help bhi le sakti hain.

Thik hai. Writ petition me exactly kya‑kya mention karna chahiye? Abhi FIR register hai, POCSO case number bhi hai, lekin threats ke bare me sirf ek general complaint diya hai. Kya witness ke roop me [Victim] ki safety ko highlight kar sakti hoon? Kya medical record, bail order, ya school details bhi attach karne chahiye, ya wo risky hoga?

Petition me:
- FIR number, POCSO Special Court case number, Sections (POCSO Sections 3–10, relevant IPC 354/376, etc.)
- Threat incidents ka date/time, kisne ki, kis tarah se
- Aapki written representation to SP / AWPS (with acknowledgement)

Annexure ke liye: FIR, charge‑sheet summary, bail order (agar accused ko bail mili hai), medical record (basic, bina unnecessary details). School ka naam judge ko bata sakti hain, par public documents me full address limited rakhne ki request kar sakti hain. Zaroor highlight kariye ki [Victim] key witness hai, isliye special protection chahiye.

Agar accused baar‑baar contact karne ki koshish kare, compromise ke liye pressure dale ya gawahon ko daraaye, to kya sirf police protection kaafi hai? Kya is base par unka bail cancel karwane ka option hota hai? Ye application Special POCSO Court me dalein ya High Court me?

Agar [Accused] witness ko influence karne ki koshish kare, daraye, ya condition violate kare, to do parallel steps possible hain:

1) Special POCSO Court me application de sakte hain bail cancellation / modification ke liye, yeh bolkar ki accused intimidation kar raha hai.
2) Public Prosecutor ko written me sab details dijiye, taaki woh Court me raise kare.

Court, especially jab charges POCSO + IPC 354/376 type serious ho, to strict conditions laga sakti hai (area se door rehna, contact na karna, etc.). Outcome guarantee nahi hoti, par yeh strong ground hota hai.

Mujhe [Victim] ki daily safety ka bhi tension hai—school aana‑jana, court me statement dena, etc. Kya law me koi provision hai ki uska statement ek hi baar ho, baar‑baar cross na ho? Aur agar ghar me bahut risk ho to kya kisi government shelter ya aisi jagah temporarily rakha ja sakta hai?

POCSO ke hisaab se child‑friendly procedure mandatory hai:
- Statement ideally ek hi baar, specially Court me (CrPC Section 164 statement, POCSO Section 24), taaki repeated trauma na ho.
- In‑camera proceedings hoti hain, public ko allow nahi kiya jata.

Agar ghar me serious risk ho, to District Child Protection Unit / CWC (Child Welfare Committee) ke through temporary shelter home ya fit facility arrange ho sakti hai, JJ Act ke under. Aap Childline 1098, local DLSA / Legal Aid office aur police se milkar safety plan, school route protection, escort, etc. ke liye written request de sakti hain.

statutes_cited: Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act

turns.text: Maine ek High Court ka case padha jahan 12 saal ki ladki ke saath rape aur murder hua tha aur court ne death sentence diya. Mujhe samajhna hai, aise matter me kaun‑kaun se law apply hote hain? POCSO ka role kya hota hai, especially jab victim minor ho? Aur court age kaise decide karta hai, birth certificate ya school record se? Kya sirf witness ki baat se bhi age maan sakte hain?

Aise case me victim 18 se kam ho to automatically “child” maana jata hai, POCSO apply hota hai.

Main laws usually:
- POCSO: especially Sections 3–6 (penetrative sexual assault, aggravated)
- IPC: Section 376 (rape), 302 (murder), kabhi‑kabhi 354, 354A bhi

Age prove karne ke liye court normally pehle: birth certificate, school record, hospital record dekhta hai. Agar yeh nahi ho to medical opinion + guardian/witness evidence dekha jata hai. Sirf oral statement se normally kaafi nahi; court objective record ko zyada importance deta hai.

Agar kisi [Victim] ke saath aisa kuch ho jaye, to family ko turant practically kya steps lene chahiye? FIR kaise file karein, kya specifically POCSO bolna zaroori hai? Aur police aur doctors ko child ke rights protect karne ke liye kya follow karna hota hai?

Turanat steps:
1. Nearest police station ya 112 par call karke FIR register karwana. Complaint me bata dein ki [Victim] minor hai; POCSO mention karna helpful hai, par law ke hisaab se police khud bhi POCSO add kare.
2. POCSO Section 19: har adult ka duty hai offence report karna.
3. Turant medical examination government hospital me; child friendly manner, preferably lady doctor.
4. Statement police ke samne aur phir Magistrate ke samne (POCSO Section 24), without harassment.
5. Childline 1098 ya local NGO se psycho‑social support lena.

Kya aise serious POCSO rape‑cum‑murder cases me compromise possible hota hai ya saza kam karwa sakte hain? Aur death sentence wale cases me appeal ka process kya hota hai, High Court aur Supreme Court ka role kya hai? Victim family ka kya role rehta hai isme?

POCSO aur IPC 376 + 302 jaise serious offences me compromise legally accept nahi hota; case State vs [Accused] hota hai, private samjhauta se case khatam nahi hota.

Death sentence:
- Trial court ke baad mandatory “confirmation” High Court karti hai.
- [Accused] appeal file kar sakta hai High Court, phir Supreme Court (Special Leave Petition).
- Mercy petition President/Governor ko bhi possible hai.

Victim family:
- Apna lawyer engage kar sakti hai.
- Victim compensation scheme ke through compensation apply kar sakti hai.
- Proceedings ki information lene ka right hota hai.

I am assisting an NGO monitoring a case where a 6‑year‑old [Victim], an inmate of a Home for Mentally Deficient Children, was beaten by other child inmates and died. The 7th accused is a staff member, charged under Section 75 JJ Act, and has moved for quashing before the High Court under Section 482 CrPC. On these facts, how do courts generally interpret “assault, abandonment, wilful neglect” under Section 75, especially for supervisory staff who were not physically present at the assault?

This is a deeply distressing situation, and your close monitoring can really help protect other children.

Section 75 JJ Act is usually read widely. Courts look at:

1. Was the accused “in charge of” or “having control over” the child?
2. Did they by act or omission expose the child to physical or mental suffering?
3. Was the neglect wilful (knowledge of risk, conscious disregard)?

For supervisory staff, liability often turns on:
- duty of care in the institution’s rules,
- prior complaints or patterns,
- whether they failed to prevent or respond to known risks, not mere hindsight negligence.

Here, the direct assailants are child inmates (accused 1–4). From a child‑rights perspective, how is such child‑on‑child violence in an institution usually handled? Can Section 75 still be sustained against staff when the actual beating was by co‑inmates? As an intervening NGO, what specific factual aspects should we document (registers, prior incidents, staffing patterns) to support institutional responsibility rather than treating this as only a fight among children?

Legally, accused children are treated as “children in conflict with law” under the JJ Act, with a rehabilitative focus.

Section 75 can still apply to staff if their omissions created or allowed a dangerous environment:
- chronic understaffing or lack of supervision,
- ignoring earlier bullying or violence,
- unsafe practices (e.g., mixing vulnerable children without safeguards).

As NGO, document:
- admission and medical records of [Victim],
- duty rosters, CCTV, logbooks,
- prior complaints or Childline/CWC reports,
- inspection reports, minimum standards violations.

These help show systemic neglect, not an isolated fight.

From the [Victim]’s family’s perspective, what procedural rights and options do they have while the Sessions Case is pending and the 7th accused seeks quashing? Can they, through counsel, oppose quashing, seek further investigation, or press for addition of appropriate charges (e.g., stronger JJ Act or IPC provisions if evidence supports)? Please outline practical steps they can take, including any role for the CWC, DLSA, or victim compensation mechanisms.

The family has important rights and can be supported sensitively.

Possible steps (through their lawyer/NGO):
- File an intervention/affidavit opposing quashing in the High Court.
- Before the Sessions Court, seek further investigation under Section 173(8) CrPC, if gaps exist.
- Request addition/alteration of charges under Section 216 CrPC if evidence justifies.
- Approach CWC to flag systemic risk to other children.
- Approach DLSA for free legal aid and victim compensation under Section 357A CrPC.

They can also request regular case updates and child‑sensitive handling of any surviving child witnesses.

statutes_cited: Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015 – Section 75

turns.text: please help… my 13 year old girl, something bad done by our neighbour [Accused]. police made case but they are friendly with him. case now in local court. I am very scared, I dont understand anything, I am poor, cannt travel far. how to protect my child and make sure case is serious ?

I am very sorry this happened to your child. What you describe is child sexual abuse and usually comes under the POCSO Act (for example Sections 3–10) and also some parts of the IPC like 354 or 376, depending on facts.

Steps you may consider:
- Ensure [Victim] is safe, away from [Accused].
- If police are not fair, you can complain in writing to the Superintendent of Police.
- You can also call CHILDLINE 1098, or approach the nearest Child Welfare Committee for support.

in our small town every one knows [Accused]. he has money. we are scared judge and police may listen to him only. can this case go to some other district court or big court? some people saying high court can change place but I dont know how.

Yes, in some situations the High Court can transfer a criminal case to another court if there is fear of bias or serious threat.

Under the new Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (for example Section 528) and the old Code of Criminal Procedure (Section 482), a transfer request is possible.

You would normally:
- Engage a lawyer in the High Court.
- File a transfer petition explaining threats, local influence, fear.
- Attach basic documents from the case.

The High Court then decides; no one can promise the result.
I am not educated… what papers and things they will ask for this transfer? will my child need to go to high court and tell everything again? she is already so scared from police questions.

For a transfer request, your lawyer will usually need:
- Copy of FIR
- Chargesheet or complaint
- Orders from the present court
- Any proof of threats or pressure (call recordings, messages, witnesses)
- Your affidavit explaining fear and local influence.

Normally, the High Court only hears lawyers and maybe you as a parent. The child is usually not called there. Her main detailed statement should be recorded earlier, ideally under Section 164 CrPC and as per Section 24 of the POCSO Act (child‑friendly process).
my daughter cries every night, says she doesnt want to go to court again. if we dont go, will the case close? will police put any case on us for not coming? I am very confused what is right for her.

If you stop attending, the case may become weak, and the court can issue summons or even a warrant. But the law also wants to protect the child.

Under the POCSO Act (for example Sections 33, 35, 37), the Special Court must create a child‑friendly atmosphere.

You can ask through your lawyer for:
- In‑camera (private) hearings
- Screens or separate waiting areas
- Minimal number of times the child has to appear
- Support person or counsellor under POCSO/Juvenile Justice systems.

now [Accused] family calling us. sometime they say "compromise and take money", sometime they threaten that they will harm us if we talk in court. what can I do for protection? can court or police stop them from coming near us?

Do not agree to any money compromise. Child sexual abuse cases under POCSO are serious and generally cannot be settled privately.

You may:
- Immediately report each threat to police; threats can be an offence like criminal intimidation (Section 506 IPC).
- Ask the trial court, through your lawyer, to put strict bail conditions or protection orders so [Accused] cannot contact or approach you.
- Request protection under any witness protection scheme in your State.
- In emergency, call 112 or 1098 for urgent help.

statutes_cited: Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act (general provisions)

turns.text: Sir please help, main bahut dara hua hoon. Mere against FIR ho gaya hai ki maine ek ladki ke saath galat kaam kiya jab woh chhoti thi. Ab woh meri wife hai, hamari 2 bachche hain. FIR mein likha hai uski age kam thi, par usne khud bola tha 17 ke around. Uski mummy ne kam age likhwa di. Ab mujhe samajh nahi aa raha, kya mujhe jail jana padega? Kya yeh case band ho sakta hai?

Aapka darr samajh sakta hoon, aisi situation bahut stressful hoti hai.

POCSO law ke hisaab se, agar incident ke time ladki 18 se kam thi, to consent ya baad mein shaadi general rule ke hisaab se defence nahi maana jata. Court pehle us waqt ki **age** check karegi.

Mujhe yeh clear karna hoga:
1) Incident ke time uske paas kaun‑kaun se documents the? (school certificate, Aadhaar, birth certificate?)
2) Case kis stage par hai – sirf FIR hai ya charge‑sheet bhi file ho gayi?
3) Kya aapke paas koi written proof hai ki woh 17 batati thi?

Haan sir, uska school leaving certificate mein age aisi hai ki us time lagbhag 18 banti thi, Aadhaar bhi 18 ke aas‑paas show karta hai. FIR mein sirf maa ne 14 likhwa diya. Ab humne mandir mein shaadi ki thi, ab dono family normal ho gaye, wife bhi mere favour mein hai.

Kya aisa ho sakta hai ki High Court se FIR hi khatam ho jaye? Woh bhi bol rahi hai ki uski marzi se relation tha, ab bachche bhi hain.

Agar school certificate, Aadhaar wagairah se us samay ki age 18 ke kareeb dikh rahi hai, to yeh aapke favour mein strong point ho sakta hai.

Aap log High Court mein **quashing petition** file kar sakte hain (generally CrPC Section 482 ke under). Court aksar yeh cheezein dekhti hai:
- Official age‑proof documents
- Ladki ka 164 CrPC statement, kya woh bolan ki relation voluntary tha
- Aap logon ki shaadi aur bachchon ka welfare

Lekin court FIR zaroor khatam karegi, yeh koi guarantee nahi di ja sakti. Iske liye ek experienced criminal/POCSO lawyer zaroor rakhiye.

Abhi sabse zyada darr yeh hai ki police mujhe kabhi bhi pakad legi kya? Main roz job pe jata hoon, raat ko neend nahi aati. Case mein POCSO laga hua hai, rape type ka bhi kuch likha hai. Kya main pehle se bail le sakta hoon? Agar arrest ho gaya to kya hoga sir?

POCSO aur rape‑related IPC Sections (jaise 376 IPC, 3/4 POCSO) non‑bailable aur serious hote hain, isliye aapko **bail strategy** turant plan karni chahiye.

Aap yeh steps soch sakte hain:
1) Jaldi se ek local criminal/POCSO lawyer se milke **anticipatory bail** (pre‑arrest bail) ke liye apply karein.
2) Saare age‑proof documents, shaadi ka proof, bachchon ke birth certificates ready rakhein.
3) Police investigation mein cooperate karein, complainant ya witness ko bilkul influence/pressure na karein.

Agar anticipatory bail na mile aur arrest ho, to aapka lawyer **regular bail** file karega.

Sir meri wife ko police ne bola hai ki statement dena padega. Woh bhi dara hua hai. Usko laga ki usko bhi punish kar denge ki usne ghar se bhaag ke shaadi ki thi. Court mein uska statement kaise hota hai? Agar woh mere favour mein bole to kuch help hoga kya?

Aapki wife ko is case mein generally **victim/witness** maana jata hai, accused nahi, isliye usko punish karne ka normal rule nahi hota.

Process usually aisa hota hai:
1) Police 161 CrPC ke under uska statement legi.
2) Magistrate ke saamne **164 CrPC statement** record hota hai – yeh zyada important hota hai.

POCSO mein technically 18 se kam age par consent legally valid nahi maana jata, lekin jab woh aaj adult hai, Court uska voluntary relationship, shaadi aur bachchon ke welfare ko overall context mein consider kar sakti hai. Zaroori hai ki woh bina pressure sach bole.

Sir mere bachchon ke baare mein bhi tension hai. Koi social worker type ghar aaya tha, bola children ke bare mein inquiry hogi. Kya mere bachche ko le ja sakte hain? JJ waala koi law hai na, mujhe samajh nahi aa raha, main bahut ghabra gaya hoon.

JJ Act (Juvenile Justice Act) tab apply hota hai jab bachche **“child in need of care and protection”** lagein – jaise abuse, neglect, ya unsafe environment. Sirf isliye ki aapke khilaf POCSO/IPC case hai, normally bachche ko turant le jana ka rule nahi hai.

Aap yeh karein:
- Jo bhi Child Welfare Committee/social worker aaye, unse calmly cooperate karein.
- Bachchon ke school, medical, birth certificate jaise documents ready rakhein.
- Ghar ka environment safe rakhein, bachchon ko case ke bare mein forcefully kuch bolne ko mat sikhaiye.
- Zarurat ho to **legal aid** office ya trusted lawyer se guidance lijiye.
- Agar kabhi lage bachchon ko khatra hai, aap ya aapki wife **Childline 1098** par bhi call kar sakte hain.

statutes_cited: Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012 - Section 3
Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012 - Section 4
Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012 - Sections 7-10 (general reference)
Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012 - Section 19
Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012 - Section 24
Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015 - general provisions on Child in Need of Care and Protection

turns.text: Mera case kuch Joseph wale Karnataka case jaisa hai. Meri estranged wife ne mere against POCSO ke under complaint kar di, jabki main lagta hai yeh sab custody dispute ki wajah se hai. Bachcha [Minor] ab uske paas hai. Main samajhna chahta hoon ki aise situation me court evidence kaise dekhta hai, especially jab pehle se custody ka jhagda chal raha ho. Mujhe kya steps lene chahiye taaki meri side clear ho aur bachche ka welfare bhi safe rahe?

Aap jo situation describe kar rahe hain, woh emotionally bhi heavy hai aur legally bhi serious, kyunki POCSO ek strict law hai. Court normally yeh dekhega: pehle ka custody arrangement kya tha, allegation ka timing kya hai, koi digital records / emails / messages jo dispute dikhate ho, aur bachche ki current condition. Aapko turant ek criminal lawyer (POCSO experience wala) hire karna chahiye, bail ke liye prepare hona chahiye, aur saare relevant documents, earlier orders, chats, emails properly collect karke lawyer ko dene chahiye.

Mujhe thoda clear bata dijiye, aise case me kaun kaun se law apply ho sakte hain? Agar allegation mainly touching-type incident ka hai, koi medical report ya eyewitness nahi hai, sirf [Minor] ka statement hai jo shayad mother influence kar rahi ho, toh police FIR kis basis par karegi? Sirf allegation se bhi POCSO case strong ban jata hai kya?

Generally, aise allegation POCSO Act ke Section 3–4 (penetrative) ya Section 7–8 (sexual assault/touching) ke under dikhaye ja sakte hain, kabhi‑kabhi IPC Section 354, 354A, 376 bhi add karte hain. FIR ke liye sirf [Minor] ka statement bhi enough hota hai; medical ya eyewitness zaroori condition nahi hai. POCSO Section 19 ke hisaab se reporting mandatory hai. Baad me court evidence ko detail me assess karega: consistency of statement, surrounding circumstances, custody dispute history, delay in complaint, sab cheezein dekhi jaati hain.

Samajh gaya. Ab bail ke point par bataiye. Jab custody dispute ka clear history hai, aur pehle kabhi mere khilaaf child safety ki complaint nahi thi, toh bail decide karte waqt judge kya factors dekhega? Kya yeh argument ki wife ne POCSO ka misuse kiya hai, custody ke liye, bail me helpful ho sakta hai?

Bail me court yeh cheezein dekhega: allegation ka nature (POCSO Section 3–10 kaunsa part laga hai), complaint ka timing, pehle se koi similar complaint hai ya nahi, custody dispute aur uske orders, chats/emails jo dikhayein ki dispute pehle se chal raha tha, aapka criminal record, aur witness ko influence karne ka risk. Misuse ka argument sirf ek factor hai; court child safety ko priority deta hai. Lekin custody‑related background kabhi‑kabhi bail ke favour me reasonable doubt create kar sakta hai.

Mujhe [Minor] ke statement ke process ka bhi tension hai. Agar mother influence kar rahi hai, toh bachcha psychologically affect ho sakta hai. POCSO me child ka statement kaise record hota hai? Kya main court se request kar sakta hoon ki neutral environment me, counsellor ke saath statement ho, taaki bachche par pressure kam ho?

POCSO child‑friendly procedure kaafi clearly deta hai. Section 24 ke hisaab se statement non‑threatening environment me, usually child ke convenient place par, female officer ya trained officer se record hona chahiye, repeated questioning avoid ki jaati hai. Baad me special court, support person / counsellor ko involve kar sakta hai. Aap apne lawyer ke through court me application de sakte hain ki [Minor] ka statement neutral setting me, psychologist/counsellor ke presence me ho, unnecessary contact parents se avoid ho, taaki influence aur trauma dono kam ho.

Abhi practical step kya honge? FIR already register ho chuki hai, police ne mujhe notice bheja hai statement ke liye. Main dar raha hoon ki kuch galat bol doon. Kya main police ke saamne detail me apni custody dispute wali side explain karun? Aur kya electronic chats, earlier family court orders abhi hi dene chahiye?

Police ke saamne bina lawyer ke detailed statement dene se bachna better hai. Apne lawyer ke saath jaayiye, basic facts clear, consistent rakhiye, exaggeration ya guess work mat kijiye. Custody dispute, pehle ke family court orders, chats/emails jo dikhate hain ki allegation ka timing suspicious hai, yeh sab documents ka set bana kar lawyer ko dijiye; woh decide karega kya police ko abhi dena hai ya bail/court stage par rely karna hai. Complainant ya [Minor] se direct contact avoid kijiye, sirf legal channels use kijiye.

statutes_cited.name: Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012

turns.text: Sir please help. Mera beta [Victim] police station me custody me mar gaya, aur uske saath jo do ladke the, woh bol rahe hain ki unko andar bahut maar-pitai aur gandi cheezein ki gayi. Mujhe kuch samajh nahi aa raha, police khud par case kaise karein? Kya yeh bas normal investigation hoga ya kuch alag process hota hai jab police hi galat karti hai?

Mujhe bahut afsos hai jo aapke saath hua, yeh bahut traumatic situation hai.

Jab police custody me death ya sexual abuse hota hai, toh alag seriousness se dekha jata hai. Agar [Victim] minor tha toh sexual abuse POCSO Act (Sections 3–10, 19, 24) ke under aata hai; agar bada tha toh IPC Sections 354, 354A, 376 lag sakte hain, death ke liye IPC Section 302 bhi ho sakta hai.

Aap: High Court me writ, CBI/special investigation ki demand, NHRC/State Human Rights Commission ko complaint de sakte hain.

Sir lawyer ne bola tha ki court se bol sakte hain ki sirf maar-pitai nahi, murder ka charge bhi lagna chahiye, par judge maanenge ya nahi, darr lag raha hai. Yeh sexual torture wali baat bhi un do ladko ne boli hai, par woh bhi dare hue hain. Main kya bol sakta hoon court me ya application me, taaki yeh sab cheezein properly add ho jayein?

Aapka darr bilkul samajh mein aata hai. Court ko impress karne ki zimmedari mainly aapke lawyer ki hogi, aapko sab sach clearly batana hai.

1) Aapke lawyer written application de sakte hain ki available evidence ke basis par additional charge "murder" ka add ho, jo IPC Section 302 ke under aata hai.
2) Sexual torture ke liye:
   - agar victim/saath wale ladke minor the, toh POCSO Sections 3–10 add karne ki request,
   - agar adult, toh IPC Sections 354/376.

Aapko detail facts, medical papers, post-mortem report, statements sab lawyer ko dene chahiye.

मैं एक सामाजिक संस्था के साथ काम कर रहा हूं। हमारे पास पुडुचेरी जैसा मामला है, जहाँ कई आरोपी पर बच्चे की अवैध खरीद, गलत तरीके से रोकना, गम्भीर प्रवेशिक लैंगिक अत्याचार, बच्चे पर क्रूरता और नशीला पदार्थ देने के आरोप हैं। पुलिस ने POCSO की धारा 6, 10, 17 और एससी-एसटी अधिनियम की धारा 3(1)(w)(i) लगाई हैं। अपील स्तर पर मैं जानना चाहता हूं कि बच्चे के बयान और सुरक्षा के लिए POCSO की धारा 19, 24 और CrPC के तहत क्या मुख्य प्रक्रिया और सुरक्षा प्रावधान हैं?

आप बहुत महत्वपूर्ण काम कर रहे हैं, और ऐसे मामलों में बच्चे की सुरक्षा सर्वोपरि है।

संक्षेप में:
1. POCSO धारा 19: किसी भी व्यक्ति को ऐसी जानकारी होने पर अनिवार्य सूचना पुलिस को देनी होती है।
2. CrPC धारा 154: इस सूचना के आधार पर FIR दर्ज होगी।
3. CrPC धारा 164: बच्चे का बयान न्यायिक दण्डाधिकारी के सामने, सुरक्षित और सहायक माहौल में दर्ज किया जा सकता है।
4. POCSO धारा 24: पुलिस पूछताछ बाल हितैषी तरीके से, अधिमानतः महिला अधिकारी द्वारा, विद्यालय या घर जैसे सुरक्षित स्थान पर होगी।

धन्यवाद। इस मामले में आरोप है कि बच्ची को पहले बहला–फुसलाकर लाया गया, फिर कमरे में बंद रखा गया और नशीला पदार्थ देकर शारीरिक शोषण किया गया।

क्या ऐसी “अवैध बाल आपूर्ति” और कमरे में बंद करके रखने पर सामान्यतः IPC की धारा 370, 344 इत्यादि भी जोड़ी जा सकती हैं? और नशीला पदार्थ देने की स्थिति में कौन‑सी धारा (जैसे 328) प्रासंगिक रहती है? यदि आरोप पत्र में ये धाराएँ नहीं लगीं, तो क्या विशेष न्यायालय CrPC के तहत आरोपों में संशोधन कर सकता है?

आपके वर्णन के आधार पर सामान्य कानूनी स्थिति यह हो सकती है (अन्तिम निर्णय न्यायालय का होगा):

1. अवैध रूप से बच्चे को लाना–ले जाना और शोषण करना, सामान्यतः IPC धारा 370 या बाल तस्करी से जुड़ी धाराओं के तहत आ सकता है।
2. कमरे में बंद रखना, IPC धारा 344 (दीर्घ काल के लिए गलत बन्दीकरण) के तहत आ सकता है।
3. नशीला या हानिकारक पदार्थ देकर अपराध करना, IPC धारा 328 के तहत आ सकता है।
4. यदि आरोप पत्र में कुछ धारा कम हैं, तो विशेष न्यायालय CrPC धारा 216 के तहत आरोपों में संशोधन कर सकता है, बशर्ते दोनों पक्षों को सुनकर न्यायपूर्ण मौका दे।

अब अपील में आरोपी जमानत की मांग कर रहे हैं। पीड़ित बच्ची अनुसूचित जाति समुदाय से है, इसलिए एससी‑एसटी अधिनियम भी लगा है।

क्या इस प्रकार के अपराध में एससी‑एसटी अधिनियम के चलते अग्रिम जमानत पर पूर्ण रोक मानी जाती है? और नियमित जमानत पर विशेष न्यायालय को किन बिन्दुओं (जैसे POCSO धारा 33(7), गवाहों को डराने का खतरा, पुनरावृत्ति का जोखिम) को खास तौर पर देखना चाहिए, विशेषकर जब बच्ची की गवाही अभी दर्ज नहीं हुई है?

सामान्यतः:

1. एससी‑एसटी अधिनियम में धारा 18 के अनुसार, इस अधिनियम के अपराधों में अग्रिम जमानत पर रोक मानी जाती है; फिर भी प्रत्येक मामला न्यायालय तथ्यों के आधार पर देखता है।
2. नियमित जमानत पर विशेष न्यायालय को विशेष रूप से देखना चाहिए:
   - POCSO धारा 33(7): क्या आरोपी के बाहर रहने पर अपराध की पुनरावृत्ति या बच्चे पर प्रभाव का खतरा है।
   - क्या आरोपी गवाहों, विशेषकर पीड़ित बच्ची और उसके परिवार, को डराने या प्रभावित करने की स्थिति में है।
   - बच्ची की गवाही अभी दर्ज न होने पर, क्या जमानत से साक्ष्य नष्ट होने का जोखिम बढ़ेगा।

व्यावहारिक स्तर पर हमारी संस्था पीड़ित पक्ष की मदद करना चाहती है।

1. क्या हम DLSA से विशेष पीड़ित मुआवजा और स्वतंत्र कानूनी सहायता के लिए आवेदन करा सकते हैं, जबकि मामला POCSO और एससी‑एसटी दोनों के तहत है?
2. क्या बाल कल्याण समिति के माध्यम से बच्चे के लिए सुरक्षित आश्रय और मनो–सामाजिक सहयोग की मांग करना उचित रहेगा?
3. क्या अदालत से यह प्रार्थना की जा सकती है कि बच्ची की गवाही दृश्य माध्यम से या पर्दे के पीछे लेकर, आरोपी से सीधा सामना न कराया जाए?

आपके कदम बहुत महत्वपूर्ण हैं:

1. आप पीड़ित पक्ष की ओर से जिला विधिक सेवा प्राधिकरण (DLSA) में निःशुल्क अधिवक्ता और पीड़ित मुआवजा योजना के लिए आवेदन करवा सकते हैं; POCSO और एससी‑एसटी दोनों मामलों में प्रासंगिक है।
2. किशोर न्याय अधिनियम के तहत बाल कल्याण समिति के समक्ष बच्चे के लिए सुरक्षा, आश्रय और मनो‑सामाजिक सहयोग का निवेदन उचित और आवश्यक है।
3. POCSO की धाराएँ 33 और 36 के अनुसार, आप विशेष न्यायालय से प्रार्थना कर सकते हैं कि बच्ची की गवाही अलग कक्ष, परदे या दृश्य माध्यम से हो, ताकि आरोपी से सीधा सामना न हो और बच्ची सुरक्षित महसूस करे।

statutes_cited: किशोर न्याय अधिनियम (बाल कल्याण समिति संबंधी प्रावधान)

turns.text: दीदी कृपया मदद करो, मेरा दिमाग काम नहीं कर रहा। मैं अपनी छोटी बच्ची लेकर मायके आ गयी थी, पति से झगड़ा था। बाद में पता चला उसने नशे में बच्ची के साथ गंदी हरकत की। मैंने पुलिस में FIR कराई। अब वो कह रहा है कोर्ट FIR रद्द कर देगी, उसने कोई गलती नहीं की। मुझे बहुत डर लग रहा है, क्या सच में ऐसा हो सकता है?

आपकी बच्ची के साथ जो भी हुआ, वह बहुत गंभीर बात है, मैं आपके डर को समझती हूँ। बच्चों के साथ यौन शोषण पर POCSO अधिनियम और IPC की सख्त धाराएँ लगती हैं। FIR रद्द करना इतना आसान नहीं होता। न्यायालय पहले पुलिस की जाँच, आपके बयान, बच्ची के बयान और बाक़ी सबूत देखता है। आपकी बात सुने बिना कोई भी आदेश नहीं होता। अभी आप सच्चाई पर डटी रहें और अपने वकील तथा सरकारी अभियोजक से नियमित बात करती रहें।

मुझे सबसे ज़्यादा डर इस बात का है कि क्या मुझे भी बार–बार अदालत में बोलना पड़ेगा? मेरी बच्ची तो बहुत डर जाती है, वह तो ठीक से बोल भी नहीं पाती। पुलिस ने एक‑दो बार पूछा था। क्या उसे बार‑बार बयान देना होगा? मैं नहीं चाहती कि वो और टूट जाए।

आपकी चिंता बिल्कुल सही है। आमतौर पर पहले पुलिस बयान लेती है, फिर मजिस्ट्रेट के सामने CrPC की धारा 164 के तहत बयान हो सकता है, ताकि बार‑बार न पूछना पड़े। POCSO के तहत बच्चे का बयान दोस्ताना माहौल में, बिना डराए लिया जाना चाहिए। न्यायालय कोशिश करती है कि बच्चे को कम से कम बार बुलाया जाए। आप यह साफ‑साफ जाँच अधिकारी और न्यायाधीश को बता सकती हैं कि बच्ची बहुत डरी हुई है, उसे संवेदनशील तरीके से ही सुना जाए।

घर वाले बोल रहे हैं कि मामला वापस ले लो, वरना बदनामी होगी, बच्ची की शादी भी मुश्किल हो जाएगी। कह रहे हैं पति ने गलती की, लेकिन घर का मामला है, चुप रहो। मैं बहुत दबाव में हूँ। क्या मैं FIR वापस ले सकती हूँ अगर चाहूँ तो?

ऐसे मामलों को आमतौर पर निजी समझौते से खत्म नहीं किया जाता, क्योंकि POCSO के तहत यह अपराध समाज के ख़िलाफ भी माना जाता है। मामला आमतौर पर राज्य बनाम आरोपी होता है, केवल आपकी इच्छा से स्वतः समाप्त नहीं होता। यदि आप पर परिवार से दबाव या धमकी है, तो यह बात पुलिस, अभियोजक या न्यायाधीश को ज़रूर बताएँ। आप अपने ज़िले की DLSA से निःशुल्क वकील और सहायता माँग सकती हैं, वे भी आपको सुरक्षा और कानूनी विकल्प समझाएँगे।
पति कह रहा है कि वो तो नशे में था, होश में नहीं था, बस एक बार की गलती है, कोर्ट मान जाएगी और बोलेगी झूठा केस है, FIR रद्द कर देगी। मुझे समझ नहीं आ रहा, क्या नशे का बहाना सच में चल जाता है अदालत में?

कानून में नशे को आमतौर पर बचाव का आसान बहाना नहीं माना जाता, ख़ासकर जब बात बच्चे के साथ यौन शोषण जैसी गंभीर बात की हो। FIR रद्द तभी होती है जब पहली नज़र में ही साफ लगे कि कोई अपराध बना ही नहीं या शिकायत पूरी तरह झूठी है। यहाँ पुलिस जाँच, बच्ची का बयान, मेडिकल और अन्य सबूत देखे जाएँगे। आप बस सच पर डटी रहें, अभियोजक या अपने वकील से मिलकर सुनवाई के लिए तैयारी करती रहें।

ठीक है, थोड़ा हिम्मत आ रही है। आप बताइए मुझे अभी तुरंत क्या‑क्या करना चाहिए? बच्ची रात में चौंक जाती है, रोने लगती है, स्कूल भी नहीं जाना चाहती। मुझे समझ नहीं आ रहा, पहले उसकी भलाई देखूँ या केस की भागदौड़ करूँ।

सबसे पहले आपकी बच्ची की सुरक्षा और मन की हालत ज़रूरी है।
1. उसे आरोपी से पूरी तरह दूर रखें।
2. किसी सरकारी अस्पताल या वन‑स्टॉप केन्द्र में चिकित्सकीय जाँच और मनोवैज्ञानिक परामर्श के लिए जाएँ।
3. पुलिस जाँच में सहयोग करें, जो भी गवाह हों, उनके नाम पुलिस को दें।
4. यदि कोई धमका रहा हो तो तुरंत लिखित शिकायत दें।
5. Childline 1098 या DLSA से संपर्क कर भावनात्मक, कानूनी और अन्य सहायता लें।

मैं [Accused] के पिता हूँ, मेरे बेटे की दूसरी पत्नी [Victim] की माँ है। मेरी चेन्नई वाली संपत्ति अपनी नाबालिग बेटी के नाम कराने का दबाव था, मैंने मना किया तो बहू ने मेरे खिलाफ FIR कर दी, और सुना है POCSO भी जोड़ा गया है। मेरे ऊपर किसी तरह के शारीरिक शोषण का झूठा आरोप लगाया गया है। क्या ऐसे मामले में सच में POCSO लग सकता है? और क्या FIR को उच्च न्यायालय से रद्द कराना सम्भव है?
